he.text: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said O Sañjaya what happened when my sons and the sons of Pāṇḍu assembled for battle at the holy place of Kurukṣetra

ar.text: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said O Sañjaya what happened when my sons and the sons of Pāṇḍu assembled for battle at the holy place of Kurukṣetra

tr.text: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said O Sañjaya what happened when my sons and the sons of Pāṇḍu assembled for battle at the holy place of Kurukṣetra

sw.text: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said O Sañjaya what happened when my sons and the sons of Pāṇḍu assembled for battle at the holy place of Kurukṣetra

he.text: Sañjaya said Seeing the Pāṇḍavas’ soldiers and armies arrayed for battle King Duryodhan approached Droṇāchārya and said

ar.text: Sañjaya said Seeing the Pāṇḍavas’ soldiers and armies arrayed for battle King Duryodhan approached Droṇāchārya and said

tr.text: Sañjaya said Seeing the Pāṇḍavas’ soldiers and armies arrayed for battle King Duryodhan approached Droṇāchārya and said

sw.text: Sañjaya said Seeing the Pāṇḍavas’ soldiers and armies arrayed for battle King Duryodhan approached Droṇāchārya and said

he.text: O my teacher please observe the great armies of the Pāṇḍavas arranged in military formation by your brilliant disciple Dhṛṣṭadyumna

ar.text: O my teacher please observe the great armies of the Pāṇḍavas arranged in military formation by your brilliant disciple Dhṛṣṭadyumna

tr.text: O my teacher please observe the great armies of the Pāṇḍavas arranged in military formation by your brilliant disciple Dhṛṣṭadyumna

sw.text: O my teacher please observe the great armies of the Pāṇḍavas arranged in military formation by your brilliant disciple Dhṛṣṭadyumna

he.text: O best of the twice-born please consider too the foremost amongst us the commanders of my armies I submit their names before you

ar.text: O best of the twice-born please consider too the foremost amongst us the commanders of my armies I submit their names before you

tr.text: O best of the twice-born please consider too the foremost amongst us the commanders of my armies I submit their names before you

sw.text: O best of the twice-born please consider too the foremost amongst us the commanders of my armies I submit their names before you

he.text: The strength of our army headed by Bhīṣma seems inestimable yet the strength of the Pāṇḍavas’ army protected by Bhīma is estimable

ar.text: The strength of our army headed by Bhīṣma seems inestimable yet the strength of the Pāṇḍavas’ army protected by Bhīma is estimable

tr.text: The strength of our army headed by Bhīṣma seems inestimable yet the strength of the Pāṇḍavas’ army protected by Bhīma is estimable

sw.text: The strength of our army headed by Bhīṣma seems inestimable yet the strength of the Pāṇḍavas’ army protected by Bhīma is estimable

he.text: Now take up your posts at the strategic points of the formation in support of Bhīṣma

ar.text: Now take up your posts at the strategic points of the formation in support of Bhīṣma

tr.text: Now take up your posts at the strategic points of the formation in support of Bhīṣma

sw.text: Now take up your posts at the strategic points of the formation in support of Bhīṣma

he.text: Then with a war-cry like the roar of a lion the venerable elder Bhīṣma loudly blew his conchshell to encourage Duryodhan

ar.text: Then with a war-cry like the roar of a lion the venerable elder Bhīṣma loudly blew his conchshell to encourage Duryodhan

tr.text: Then with a war-cry like the roar of a lion the venerable elder Bhīṣma loudly blew his conchshell to encourage Duryodhan

sw.text: Then with a war-cry like the roar of a lion the venerable elder Bhīṣma loudly blew his conchshell to encourage Duryodhan

he.text: At once there arose a tumultuous vibration of conchshells kettledrums hand-drums bugles and the resounding of many instruments

ar.text: At once there arose a tumultuous vibration of conchshells kettledrums hand-drums bugles and the resounding of many instruments

tr.text: At once there arose a tumultuous vibration of conchshells kettledrums hand-drums bugles and the resounding of many instruments

sw.text: At once there arose a tumultuous vibration of conchshells kettledrums hand-drums bugles and the resounding of many instruments

he.text: Then Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna in a magnificent chariot drawn by white horses blew their divine conchshells

ar.text: Then Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna in a magnificent chariot drawn by white horses blew their divine conchshells

tr.text: Then Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna in a magnificent chariot drawn by white horses blew their divine conchshells

sw.text: Then Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna in a magnificent chariot drawn by white horses blew their divine conchshells

he.text: Hṛṣīkeś blew His conchshell Pāñchajanya Dhanañjaya sounded his the Devadatta and Bhīmasen the performer of mighty tasks blew the conchshell Pauṇḍra

ar.text: Hṛṣīkeś blew His conchshell Pāñchajanya Dhanañjaya sounded his the Devadatta and Bhīmasen the performer of mighty tasks blew the conchshell Pauṇḍra

tr.text: Hṛṣīkeś blew His conchshell Pāñchajanya Dhanañjaya sounded his the Devadatta and Bhīmasen the performer of mighty tasks blew the conchshell Pauṇḍra

sw.text: Hṛṣīkeś blew His conchshell Pāñchajanya Dhanañjaya sounded his the Devadatta and Bhīmasen the performer of mighty tasks blew the conchshell Pauṇḍra

he.text: King Yudhiṣṭhir the son of Kuntī blew the Anantavijay while Nakul and Sahadev blew the conchshells Sughoṣa and Maṇipuṣpaka

ar.text: King Yudhiṣṭhir the son of Kuntī blew the Anantavijay while Nakul and Sahadev blew the conchshells Sughoṣa and Maṇipuṣpaka

tr.text: King Yudhiṣṭhir the son of Kuntī blew the Anantavijay while Nakul and Sahadev blew the conchshells Sughoṣa and Maṇipuṣpaka

sw.text: King Yudhiṣṭhir the son of Kuntī blew the Anantavijay while Nakul and Sahadev blew the conchshells Sughoṣa and Maṇipuṣpaka

he.text: Resounding throughout the land and heavens the tremendous vibration of those conchshells pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

ar.text: Resounding throughout the land and heavens the tremendous vibration of those conchshells pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

tr.text: Resounding throughout the land and heavens the tremendous vibration of those conchshells pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

sw.text: Resounding throughout the land and heavens the tremendous vibration of those conchshells pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

he.text: O King as the battle was about to commence Arjuna whose chariot was adorned with the flag of Hanumān seeing Duryodhan and his army poised for battle took up his bow and said to Śrī Kṛṣṇa

ar.text: O King as the battle was about to commence Arjuna whose chariot was adorned with the flag of Hanumān seeing Duryodhan and his army poised for battle took up his bow and said to Śrī Kṛṣṇa

tr.text: O King as the battle was about to commence Arjuna whose chariot was adorned with the flag of Hanumān seeing Duryodhan and his army poised for battle took up his bow and said to Śrī Kṛṣṇa

sw.text: O King as the battle was about to commence Arjuna whose chariot was adorned with the flag of Hanumān seeing Duryodhan and his army poised for battle took up his bow and said to Śrī Kṛṣṇa

he.text: Sañjaya said O descendant of Bharat after the vigilant Arjuna spoke these words Śrī Kṛṣṇa the Lord of the perceptions of all living beings drew the grand chariot between both armies

ar.text: Sañjaya said O descendant of Bharat after the vigilant Arjuna spoke these words Śrī Kṛṣṇa the Lord of the perceptions of all living beings drew the grand chariot between both armies

tr.text: Sañjaya said O descendant of Bharat after the vigilant Arjuna spoke these words Śrī Kṛṣṇa the Lord of the perceptions of all living beings drew the grand chariot between both armies

sw.text: Sañjaya said O descendant of Bharat after the vigilant Arjuna spoke these words Śrī Kṛṣṇa the Lord of the perceptions of all living beings drew the grand chariot between both armies

he.text: before Bhīṣma Droṇa and all the chieftains of the earth The Lord then said O Pārtha behold the Kauravas assembled here for battle

ar.text: before Bhīṣma Droṇa and all the chieftains of the earth The Lord then said O Pārtha behold the Kauravas assembled here for battle

tr.text: before Bhīṣma Droṇa and all the chieftains of the earth The Lord then said O Pārtha behold the Kauravas assembled here for battle

sw.text: before Bhīṣma Droṇa and all the chieftains of the earth The Lord then said O Pārtha behold the Kauravas assembled here for battle

he.text: There within both armies Arjuna saw paternal and maternal elders grandfathers teachers brothers sons grandsons fathers-in-law friends and other well-wishers

ar.text: There within both armies Arjuna saw paternal and maternal elders grandfathers teachers brothers sons grandsons fathers-in-law friends and other well-wishers

tr.text: There within both armies Arjuna saw paternal and maternal elders grandfathers teachers brothers sons grandsons fathers-in-law friends and other well-wishers

sw.text: There within both armies Arjuna saw paternal and maternal elders grandfathers teachers brothers sons grandsons fathers-in-law friends and other well-wishers

he.text: Seeing his kinsmen present on the battlefield Arjuna the son of Kuntī was grief-stricken and overcome with pity He said

ar.text: Seeing his kinsmen present on the battlefield Arjuna the son of Kuntī was grief-stricken and overcome with pity He said

tr.text: Seeing his kinsmen present on the battlefield Arjuna the son of Kuntī was grief-stricken and overcome with pity He said

sw.text: Seeing his kinsmen present on the battlefield Arjuna the son of Kuntī was grief-stricken and overcome with pity He said

he.text: O Kṛṣṇa seeing all these relatives and friends assembled and eager for battle the strength is draining from my limbs and my mouth feels parched

ar.text: O Kṛṣṇa seeing all these relatives and friends assembled and eager for battle the strength is draining from my limbs and my mouth feels parched

tr.text: O Kṛṣṇa seeing all these relatives and friends assembled and eager for battle the strength is draining from my limbs and my mouth feels parched

sw.text: O Kṛṣṇa seeing all these relatives and friends assembled and eager for battle the strength is draining from my limbs and my mouth feels parched

he.text: I am trembling and the hairs on my body are standing on end my skin is burning and I am losing grip of my Gāṇḍīva bow

ar.text: I am trembling and the hairs on my body are standing on end my skin is burning and I am losing grip of my Gāṇḍīva bow

tr.text: I am trembling and the hairs on my body are standing on end my skin is burning and I am losing grip of my Gāṇḍīva bow

sw.text: I am trembling and the hairs on my body are standing on end my skin is burning and I am losing grip of my Gāṇḍīva bow

he.text: O Keśava I cannot maintain my composure any longer My mind is disturbed and I see bad omens

ar.text: O Keśava I cannot maintain my composure any longer My mind is disturbed and I see bad omens

tr.text: O Keśava I cannot maintain my composure any longer My mind is disturbed and I see bad omens

sw.text: O Keśava I cannot maintain my composure any longer My mind is disturbed and I see bad omens

he.text: I do not see any good in killing my kinsmen in this battle O Kṛṣṇa I have no wish for victory or to enjoy a kingdom

ar.text: I do not see any good in killing my kinsmen in this battle O Kṛṣṇa I have no wish for victory or to enjoy a kingdom

tr.text: I do not see any good in killing my kinsmen in this battle O Kṛṣṇa I have no wish for victory or to enjoy a kingdom

sw.text: I do not see any good in killing my kinsmen in this battle O Kṛṣṇa I have no wish for victory or to enjoy a kingdom

he.text: O Janārdan even if we gain sovereignty over the three worlds not to speak of ruling the earth how can we be happy if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

ar.text: O Janārdan even if we gain sovereignty over the three worlds not to speak of ruling the earth how can we be happy if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

tr.text: O Janārdan even if we gain sovereignty over the three worlds not to speak of ruling the earth how can we be happy if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

sw.text: O Janārdan even if we gain sovereignty over the three worlds not to speak of ruling the earth how can we be happy if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra

he.text: Only sin will befall us if we slay our teachers and guardians though they be our adversaries We cannot take the lives of our own relatives the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra O Mādhava how can we be happy by killing our kinsmen

ar.text: Only sin will befall us if we slay our teachers and guardians though they be our adversaries We cannot take the lives of our own relatives the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra O Mādhava how can we be happy by killing our kinsmen

tr.text: Only sin will befall us if we slay our teachers and guardians though they be our adversaries We cannot take the lives of our own relatives the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra O Mādhava how can we be happy by killing our kinsmen

sw.text: Only sin will befall us if we slay our teachers and guardians though they be our adversaries We cannot take the lives of our own relatives the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra O Mādhava how can we be happy by killing our kinsmen

he.text: With the decline of the dynasty its religious traditions are lost When religious practices are lost irreligion overcomes the entire dynasty

ar.text: With the decline of the dynasty its religious traditions are lost When religious practices are lost irreligion overcomes the entire dynasty

tr.text: With the decline of the dynasty its religious traditions are lost When religious practices are lost irreligion overcomes the entire dynasty

sw.text: With the decline of the dynasty its religious traditions are lost When religious practices are lost irreligion overcomes the entire dynasty

he.text: O Kṛṣṇa descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty when the dynasty is thus overcome by irreligion the women of the family become corrupt and adulterated progeny is the result

ar.text: O Kṛṣṇa descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty when the dynasty is thus overcome by irreligion the women of the family become corrupt and adulterated progeny is the result

tr.text: O Kṛṣṇa descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty when the dynasty is thus overcome by irreligion the women of the family become corrupt and adulterated progeny is the result

sw.text: O Kṛṣṇa descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty when the dynasty is thus overcome by irreligion the women of the family become corrupt and adulterated progeny is the result

he.text: Adulterated progeny forces both the family and its aggressors into a hellish existence The same fate befalls their ancestors because regular offerings of food and water to them cease

ar.text: Adulterated progeny forces both the family and its aggressors into a hellish existence The same fate befalls their ancestors because regular offerings of food and water to them cease

tr.text: Adulterated progeny forces both the family and its aggressors into a hellish existence The same fate befalls their ancestors because regular offerings of food and water to them cease

sw.text: Adulterated progeny forces both the family and its aggressors into a hellish existence The same fate befalls their ancestors because regular offerings of food and water to them cease

he.text: Such heinous acts by the destroyers of the dynasty are the cause of adulterated progeny ruining the culture of a civilized society and noble lineage

ar.text: Such heinous acts by the destroyers of the dynasty are the cause of adulterated progeny ruining the culture of a civilized society and noble lineage

tr.text: Such heinous acts by the destroyers of the dynasty are the cause of adulterated progeny ruining the culture of a civilized society and noble lineage

sw.text: Such heinous acts by the destroyers of the dynasty are the cause of adulterated progeny ruining the culture of a civilized society and noble lineage

he.text: O Janārdan we have heard that persons whose familial social and religious traditions are disrupted dwell always in hell

ar.text: O Janārdan we have heard that persons whose familial social and religious traditions are disrupted dwell always in hell

tr.text: O Janārdan we have heard that persons whose familial social and religious traditions are disrupted dwell always in hell

sw.text: O Janārdan we have heard that persons whose familial social and religious traditions are disrupted dwell always in hell

he.text: Alas what a grave sin we have resolved to commit Driven by greed for trifling royal אושר we are about to slay our own kinsmen

ar.text: Alas what a grave sin we have resolved to commit Driven by greed for trifling royal السعادة we are about to slay our own kinsmen

tr.text: Alas what a grave sin we have resolved to commit Driven by greed for trifling royal mutluluk we are about to slay our own kinsmen

sw.text: Alas what a grave sin we have resolved to commit Driven by greed for trifling royal furaha we are about to slay our own kinsmen

he.text: If unarmed and unresisting I am killed on this battlefield by the armed sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra that will be more auspicious for me

ar.text: If unarmed and unresisting I am killed on this battlefield by the armed sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra that will be more auspicious for me

tr.text: If unarmed and unresisting I am killed on this battlefield by the armed sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra that will be more auspicious for me

sw.text: If unarmed and unresisting I am killed on this battlefield by the armed sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra that will be more auspicious for me

he.text: Sañjaya said There on the battlefield speaking these words Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot his heart filled with sadness

ar.text: Sañjaya said There on the battlefield speaking these words Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot his heart filled with sadness

tr.text: Sañjaya said There on the battlefield speaking these words Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot his heart filled with sadness

sw.text: Sañjaya said There on the battlefield speaking these words Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot his heart filled with sadness
